questions.q: BOLA is ranked #1 in the OWASP API Security Top 10 because an API that checks a valid token but does not verify object ownership allows any authenticated user to access any other user's data.
options: True
False

questions.q: Which technique is the primary method for testing BOLA in an API audit?
options: Swapping object IDs between authenticated test accounts
Sending malformed JSON payloads to crash the server

questions.q: In the Peloton API breach (2021), what made the BOLA vulnerability especially severe?
options: Some endpoints required no authentication at all
Peloton stored passwords in plaintext in its database

questions.q: When auditing an API for BOLA, it is sufficient to test only the documented, publicly listed endpoints.
options: False — undocumented endpoints must also be enumerated
True — undocumented endpoints are not reachable by attackers

questions.q: A secure API response to a BOLA test attempt (User A accessing User B's object) should be:
options: HTTP 403 Forbidden with no object data returned
HTTP 200 OK with an empty response body

questions.q: Running 'git rm' on a file containing a secret fully removes the secret from the repository because no further copies exist.
options: False — the secret remains in git history on every clone and fork
True — git rm deletes the file from all branches and history

questions.q: Which HashiCorp Vault feature eliminates the risk of a stolen static database credential being used by an attacker?
options: Dynamic secrets with short TTLs generated per request
Storing secrets in an encrypted YAML file checked into git

questions.q: In the 2022 Uber breach, what was the root cause that enabled the attacker's lateral movement?
options: A hardcoded admin credential in a PowerShell script on an internal file share
An unpatched vulnerability in Uber's public-facing web application

questions.q: Secrets scanning programs must cover source code repositories, CI/CD pipeline variables, container images, file shares, and internal scripts.
options: True
False — CI/CD variables are automatically encrypted by the pipeline provider

questions.q: What is the recommended maximum secret TTL for automated systems accessing HashiCorp Vault?
options: 1 hour
24 hours

questions.q: AWS Service Control Policies (SCPs) can be overridden by an IAM administrator within the same account if their IAM policy explicitly grants the action.
options: False — SCPs override IAM policies and cannot be bypassed by in-account admins
True — IAM AdministratorAccess always takes precedence over SCPs

questions.q: In the Capital One breach (2019), which two guardrail failures allowed the exfiltration of 106 million records?
options: No Config rule caught the WAF misconfiguration and no CloudWatch alert triggered on S3 call volume
CloudTrail was disabled and the S3 bucket had public access enabled

questions.q: CloudTrail must be enabled in all AWS regions, not just us-east-1, to ensure full API activity logging.
options: True
False — us-east-1 captures global services so other regions are optional

questions.q: Which AWS control provides CONTINUOUS compliance monitoring of resources against defined policies?
options: AWS Config Rules
AWS CloudTrail

questions.q: Storing CloudTrail logs in the same account being audited is a security best practice because it simplifies log access.
options: False — a compromised admin in the same account could delete logs; cross-account storage is required
True — same-account storage ensures the audit team has fast access

questions.q: The IAM permission 'iam:CreatePolicyVersion' alone can represent a privilege escalation path to AdministratorAccess.
options: True — a user can create a new policy version granting full admin and attach it to themselves
False — CreatePolicyVersion only modifies existing policies without changing effective permissions

questions.q: In the 2022 Okta breach, what IAM principle had been violated that directly enabled the attacker's access?
options: A third-party vendor support account had broader permissions than needed for its function
Okta had failed to enable MFA on its root AWS account

questions.q: Which combination of IAM permissions creates a privilege escalation path by allowing an attacker to launch EC2 instances with privileged roles attached?
options: iam:PassRole combined with ec2:RunInstances
iam:ListRoles combined with ec2:DescribeInstances

questions.q: Access keys older than 90 days on active IAM users are considered a security finding.
options: True
False — key age is irrelevant if the key has not been used recently

questions.q: For applications, AWS IAM best practice recommends using IAM roles rather than IAM users with long-lived access keys.
options: True — roles use temporary credentials with no access keys to rotate
False — IAM users are preferred because they have a fixed identity for audit logs

questions.q: Mounting the Docker socket (/var/run/docker.sock) inside a container effectively grants that container full control over the host.
options: True
False — the socket only allows the container to view other containers, not control them

questions.q: In the 2018 Tesla Kubernetes cryptojacking incident, what initial access point did the attackers exploit?
options: A Kubernetes dashboard exposed to the internet without authentication
A container running as root that allowed privilege escalation

questions.q: Kubernetes etcd stores Secrets encrypted by default, so additional encryption configuration is not required.
options: False — Kubernetes Secrets are base64 encoded (not encrypted) by default; encryption at rest must be explicitly configured
True — etcd uses AES-256 encryption for all stored objects automatically

questions.q: Container image CVE scanning should occur in the CI/CD pipeline before images are pushed to the registry.
options: True — catching CVEs pre-registry prevents vulnerable images from ever reaching production
False — scanning after deployment is sufficient because runtime security controls compensate

questions.q: A container specification with 'runAsUser: 0' and 'privileged: true' represents which risk in a Kubernetes audit?
options: The container runs as root with full host kernel capabilities — a process escape equals root on the host node
The container has read access to the host filesystem but cannot execute processes outside its namespace

questions.q: IaC security scanning in CI/CD catches misconfigurations before infrastructure is deployed, shifting security left.
options: True
False — IaC scanning can only run after deployment to check actual resource state

questions.q: In the Toyota Connected Services breach (2023), what single automated control would have caught the misconfiguration on day one in 2013?
options: The AWS Config rule 's3-bucket-public-read-prohibited' running continuously
A manual penetration test scheduled quarterly

questions.q: A Terraform security group resource allowing ingress from '0.0.0.0/0' on port 22 is classified as what severity by tfsec?
options: HIGH — SSH open to the entire internet is an automatic significant finding
LOW — port 22 is a standard administrative port and expected to be accessible

questions.q: Which tool performs static security analysis specifically on Terraform templates?
options: tfsec or Checkov
Trivy or Grype

questions.q: Legacy cloud resources that predate IaC management do not need to be imported into IaC scanning because historical configurations are exempt from current standards.
options: False — legacy resources must be imported into IaC management and subjected to the same scanning as new deployments
True — retroactive IaC scanning is not feasible and creates unnecessary operational overhead

questions.q: SAST (Static Application Security Testing) analyzes source code without executing it, finding vulnerabilities like SQL injection before the application is built.
options: True
False — SAST requires a running application to simulate attacks against live endpoints

questions.q: The Equifax breach (2017) was enabled because the company did not have SCA (Software Composition Analysis) integrated into its CI/CD pipeline to detect the vulnerable Apache Struts dependency.
options: True — SCA would have flagged the CVE immediately when the patch was released
False — Equifax had SCA but chose to suppress the finding manually

questions.q: Who should authorize false positive suppressions in a SAST program?
options: A security engineer with documented justification
The developer who wrote the flagged code, since they best understand the context

questions.q: Which type of security testing confirms that a vulnerability found by SAST is actually exploitable in the running application?
options: DAST (Dynamic Application Security Testing)
SCA (Software Composition Analysis)

questions.q: Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) is an example of a vulnerability that SCA tools can detect because it exists in a known open-source dependency.
options: True
False — Log4Shell is a runtime behavior and cannot be detected by static dependency scanning

questions.q: The 2013 Target breach succeeded because an HVAC vendor's network access was on the same flat network as Target's point-of-sale systems.
options: True
False — the attacker pivoted through a web application vulnerability, not a flat network

questions.q: In a properly segmented AWS environment, an RDS database in a private subnet should have a security group that allows port 5432 from which source?
options: Only the application server security group ID
0.0.0.0/0, to ensure the application can always reach the database

questions.q: VPC Flow Logs must be enabled to validate network segmentation because they provide the only record of actual traffic between subnets.
options: True
False — security group rules are sufficient proof that segmentation is working without needing traffic logs

questions.q: PCI DSS requires network segmentation to reduce the scope of the cardholder data environment and limit lateral movement.
options: True
False — PCI DSS recommends segmentation but does not require it for compliance

questions.q: A database subnet with a NAT gateway route that allows outbound internet access represents what type of segmentation finding?
options: A segmentation gap — database servers should have no outbound internet route
An acceptable configuration — NAT allows outbound only and does not expose the database to inbound connections

questions.q: Database audit logs that capture only DDL and DML operations (CREATE, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) are insufficient because they miss read-based exfiltration via SELECT queries.
options: True
False — SELECT queries do not modify data so they present no compliance logging requirement

questions.q: In the Morgan Stanley decommission breach (2020), what single database control would have rendered the data on the decommissioned hardware unreadable?
options: Encryption at rest — data without the encryption key is unreadable even on recovered hardware
Column-level masking — masked fields would have appeared as placeholder values

questions.q: A Snowflake service account (SVC_ETL) granted the ACCOUNTADMIN role when it only needs INSERT access to staging tables is classified as which type of finding?
options: Critical — the account has superadmin rights, meaning any compromise grants full database access
Medium — ACCOUNTADMIN is standard for service accounts that run scheduled ETL jobs

questions.q: Row-level security (RLS) limits the data a compromised user account can access by partitioning rows based on the authenticated user's attributes.
options: True
False — RLS only applies to database administrators and does not affect regular analyst accounts

questions.q: A development database containing copies of production PII must have encryption at rest enabled, even though it is not the production environment.
options: True — any database containing sensitive data requires encryption regardless of environment label
False — development databases are excluded from encryption requirements because they are not customer-facing

questions.q: In the Equifax breach (2017), attackers went undetected for 78 days primarily because an SSL inspection appliance had an expired certificate and was not inspecting or logging HTTPS traffic.
options: True
False — the attacker used encrypted tunnels that no monitoring system could detect at the time

questions.q: How is a SIEM log coverage gap identified during an audit?
options: By comparing the SIEM log source list against the full asset inventory
By reviewing the SIEM alert backlog for systems that have never generated an alert

questions.q: PCI DSS Requirement 10 mandates a minimum log retention of 12 months, with at least 3 months immediately accessible.
options: True
False — PCI DSS requires 6 months of retention with 1 month immediately accessible

questions.q: A SIEM with 4,237 unacknowledged alerts and an MTTA of 18.4 hours indicates an effective monitoring program.
options: False — this represents alert fatigue; effective monitoring requires MTTA under 15 minutes for critical alerts
True — a large alert backlog demonstrates comprehensive detection coverage

questions.q: Verifying that log sources are actively SENDING logs — not just configured to do so — is a required step in a log coverage audit.
options: True
False — configuration verification is sufficient; active log delivery can be assumed if no errors are reported

questions.q: Zero Trust architecture operates on the principle that users and devices inside the corporate network should be trusted by default because they have already passed perimeter controls.
options: False — Zero Trust requires every access request to be authenticated and authorized regardless of network location
True — internal network position is a valid trust signal in a Zero Trust model

questions.q: The SolarWinds attack (2020) demonstrated a core weakness of perimeter-based security by exploiting which condition?
options: Implicit internal trust that allowed the attacker to move laterally once inside the perimeter
A Zero Trust misconfiguration that exposed internal APIs to the internet

questions.q: Which authentication method represents the ADVANCED maturity level for the Identity pillar in CISA's Zero Trust Maturity Model?
options: Phishing-resistant FIDO2 hardware keys replacing SMS or TOTP
Username and password with optional SMS one-time codes

questions.q: ZTNA (Zero Trust Network Access) replaces VPN by providing application-specific access without granting broad network-level access.
options: True
False — ZTNA and VPN are complementary and both are required in a mature Zero Trust architecture

questions.q: Google's BeyondCorp (2014) was significant because it was the first major enterprise implementation demonstrating that internal network location should provide no additional trust.
options: True
False — BeyondCorp still maintained a trusted internal zone for engineering workstations

questions.q: Manual compliance audits provide continuous, real-time coverage of cloud resources, whereas automated compliance checks only capture a point-in-time snapshot.
options: False — automated compliance is continuous; manual audits are point-in-time snapshots
True — human auditors can assess context that automated tools miss, making them the primary control

questions.q: Toyota's 10-year S3 public access misconfiguration (2013–2023) would have been caught immediately by which AWS service?
options: AWS Config rule 's3-bucket-public-read-prohibited' running continuously
AWS CloudTrail logging all S3 API activity

questions.q: AWS Security Hub provides a unified compliance score by aggregating findings from Config Rules, GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, and third-party tools.
options: True
False — Security Hub only aggregates GuardDuty findings and does not integrate with Config Rules

questions.q: Auto-remediation of a Config Rule finding (such as disabling a public S3 bucket) should be logged to provide an audit trail.
options: True
False — auto-remediation actions are deterministic and do not need to be logged

questions.q: A compliance automation program where only 3 of 67 Config rules have auto-remediation enabled and findings sit an average of 47 days before remediation represents a mature compliance posture.
options: False — effective compliance automation requires auto-remediation enabled for critical and high rules with prompt remediation
True — 47 days is within the acceptable remediation window for most compliance frameworks
